rationale: Profile is a non-technical marketing manager background with career spanning civil engineering and content writing at Infosys; no ML, IR, or ranking experience whatsoever.
Career is non-technical sales and marketing roles, includes two fictional-company honeypots (Pied Piper, Initech), and has zero ML or information retrieval skills.
Only 2 years of experience in project management and operations at Wipro with no ML, AI, or engineering depth; self-described marketing manager background with casual AI curiosity only.

Entire career spent at IT-services firms (TCS, Cognizant) — an explicit JD disqualifier — with DevOps/infrastructure work only; listed advanced ML skills (Feature Engineering, Computer Vision) are inconsistent with the job descriptions and appear inflated.

Dual tier_1 education (NIT Trichy + IIT Guwahati), production recommendation system at Krutrim (AI startup) using collaborative filtering, and hands-on MLOps/PEFT/LoRA skills, though lacks explicit dense-retrieval or NDCG evaluation experience and spent 36 months at IT-services firm HCL.
Career spans two fictional honeypot companies (Dunder Mifflin, Wayne Enterprises) in non-technical operations and mechanical engineering roles, with no ML or IR skills.

Solid data engineering foundation at a reputable product company (Razorpay) with Kafka/Spark pipelines, but self-admits wanting to move into ML itself; advanced Learning-to-Rank and pgvector skills listed are inconsistent with the purely data-engineering job description, suggesting inflation.
Career consists entirely of non-technical roles (operations, graphic design, sales) at multiple fictional honeypot companies (Dunder Mifflin x2, Initech) with zero engineering or ML substance.

Entire career in operations and marketing roles at IT-services firms (Wipro, Infosys) and a fictional company (Dunder Mifflin); no ML, AI, or technical engineering experience despite the top_retrieval_dense stratum label suggesting it is a retrieval trap.

Only 2.5 years of experience, all at an IT-services firm (Mindtree) doing QA/test automation — not ML or IR; pgvector listed as advanced (32 months) is inconsistent with a test-automation role and likely inflated, with AI/ML limited to self-learner side projects.
